title: Dormammu
content:
{{Otheruses|Destroyer of Worlds}} {{CharHub|Dormammu|Dormammu}} {{Character |image = Dormammu.png |real name = Dormammu{{Ref|DS}} |alias = {{Alias |codenames = The Cosmic Conqueror{{Ref|DS}}
Destroyer of Worlds{{Ref|DS}}}} |species = [[Faltine]] |citizenship = [[Dark Dimension]] |gender = Male |affiliation = {{Affiliation|ZLT}} (formerly) |movie = ''[[Doctor Strange (film)|Doctor Strange]]'' |tv series = {{Infobox List|{{Collapse|''[[Ironheart (TV series)|Ironheart]]'' (depiction & mentioned)}}}} |comic = {{Infobox List|{{Collapse|''[[Doctor Strange Prelude Infinite Comic]]'' (depiction)
''[[Avengers: Infinity War Prelude]]'' (mentioned)}}}} |book = ''[[Avengers: Infinity War: The Heroes' Journey]]'' |actor = [[Benedict Cumberbatch]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|He is the Cosmic Conqueror, the Destroyer of Worlds. A being of infinite power and endless hunger, on a quest to invade every universe and bring all worlds into his [[Dark Dimension]]. And he hungers for [[Earth]] most of all.|[[Wong]]|Doctor Strange (film)}} '''Dormammu''' is an inter-dimensional entity who rules over the [[Dark Dimension]] and the uncle of [[Clea]]. Dormammu lent his power to [[Kaecilius]] and his [[Zealots]], who desired immortality, in exchange for them merging the Dark Dimension with Earth. Before Dormammu could complete his plans, he was confronted by [[Doctor Strange]], who used the [[Time Stone]] within the [[Eye of Agamotto]] to trap Dormammu in an endless time loop. Frustrated, Dormammu agreed to leave Earth alone and take the Zealots with him in exchange for being freed from the loop. ==Biography== {{MD|Dormammu (Earth-199999)}} ===Seeking Dominance=== ====Deal with the Zealots==== [[File:Dormammu (The Multiverse Promo).png|{{AltFloat}}|Dormammu overlooks the [[Multiverse]]]] {{Quote|Learning of an infinite [[Multiverse]] includes learning of infinite dangers. And if I told you everything else that you don't already know, you'd run from [[Kamar-Taj|here]] in terror.|[[Ancient One]] to [[Doctor Strange]]|Doctor Strange (film)}} Dormammu is a primordial life form that lives within the [[Dark Dimension]]. Wielding frighteningly tremendous supernatural power of apocalyptic proportions, Dormammu sought to conquer{{Ref|DS}} every dimension of the [[Sacred Timeline|universe]],{{Ref|TAoDSitMoM}} as well as [[Earth]]. This gained him an enemy in [[Agamotto]].{{Ref|DS}} Dormammu's ancient essence has been altered so many times by the assimilation of other dimensions that his original form and his own primordial dimension are long forgotten.{{Ref|MSVD}} To achieve the conquest of Earth, he managed to employ contact with [[Kaecilius]], a rogue member of the [[Masters of the Mystic Arts]] who had become disillusioned with the [[Ancient One]]'s ideals. The two struck up a deal, with Kaecilius agreeing to gather the necessary items to enable the Dark Dimension to come to Earth. To help ensure his success, Dormammu gave Kaecilius power from the Dark Dimension when he [[Theft of the Book of Cagliostro|stole]] the [[Book of Cagliostro]].{{Ref|DS}} ====Meeting Doctor Strange==== [[File:Dormammu 3.png|{{AltFloat}}|Dormammu is confronted by [[Doctor Strange]]]] {{Quote|You will never win."
"No, but I can lose again. And again, and again, and again, forever. And that makes you my prisoner."
"No! Stop! Make this stop! Set me free!|Dormammu and [[Doctor Strange]]|Doctor Strange (film)}} As they had planned, [[Kaecilius]] had completely destroyed two of the three Sanctums and finally with the [[Hong Kong Sanctum]] vulnerable, Dormammu was now free to do as he had always desired, to fuse the [[Dark Dimension]] with [[Earth]] and destroy it. However, [[Doctor Strange]], a new member of the [[Masters of the Mystic Arts]], flew into the [[Dark Dimension]] to confront him, claiming he had come to bargain for Earth's protection. [[File:Dormammu-doctor-strange-2016.png|{{AltFloat}}|Dormammu kills [[Doctor Strange]] repeatedly]] Angered at this, Dormammu quickly killed Strange by blasting him with energy, but to his confusion, Strange reappeared on the Dark Dimension surface, repeating himself. Perplexed, Dormammu killed Strange again only to have him reappear, repeating his intention. Stunned, Dormammu demanded to know what was happening; the Doctor revealed he had used the [[Eye of Agamotto]] and the [[Time Stone]] hidden inside it to create an infinite time loop. [[File:Dormammu energy blast.png|{{AltFloat}}|Dormammu continues to kill [[Doctor Strange]]]] As Strange intended to force Dormammu to relive the moment, Dormammu decided to kill Strange only to realize that it would take forever unless he agreed to his demands. Enraged, Dormammu killed Strange again, only for him to return again. Strange explained that this was the only trap Dormammu could not escape from, since the Dark Dimension was a place beyond time itself and therefore Dormammu had no power over it. [[File:Dormammu 1.png|left|{{AltFloat}}|Dormammu accepts [[Doctor Strange]]'s orders]] Dormammu continued to kill Strange repeatedly until he could not stand the entrapment any longer and began begging Doctor Strange to end the time loop. Strange then gave Dormammu a choice that on the condition that he would banish himself and the [[Zealots]] from Earth forever. Dormammu did so, while also turning the Zealots and Kaecilius into [[Mindless Ones]] and dragging them into the Dark Dimension to live in agony forever, and kept his own word, sealing the portal between the dimensions, never to return.{{Ref|DS}} {{Clear}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== *'''Dark Dimension Magic''': As the absolute ruler of the [[Dark Dimension]], Dormammu has absolute control over its magic and the dimension itself, with the actual dimension warping to form his face and even form other body parts, such as a hand. He uses the dimension's power as a weapon, using it to constantly kill [[Doctor Strange]] over and over again, either with energy blasts or by manipulating the very ground he stood on, when the latter trapped him in an infinite time loop using the [[Time Stone]]. His influence also stretched beyond the confines of his realm to those who used his power, such as the [[Zealots]], which caused their deformities. He was also able to rapidly turn his followers into decayed, fossil like figures with glowing red symbols of the Dark Dimension on their heads, before making them fly up into the [[Dark Dimension]]. Dormammu has the power to consume entire dimensions.{{Ref|MSCE}} **'''Superhuman Strength''': Dormammu possesses immeasurable levels of strength due to his size and spacial warping abilities, shown when he manifested a colossal hand to easily crush Doctor Strange as if he were an insect. **'''Immortality''': The Dark Dimension is a timeless entity, thereby resulting in Dormammu being a primordial immortal, with the [[Ancient One]] describing him as a precursor to time itself. **'''Power Bestowal''': Dormammu bestowed mystical powers to [[Kaecilius]] and his [[Zealots]] from the Dark Dimension, making them powerful enough to challenge the strength of the [[Ancient One]] herself. They notably gained the ability to fold space without being inside the [[Mirror Dimension]], which allowed them to form [[Magic#Dark Dimension Magic|Space Shards]]. However, his bond to his followers also allowed him to absorb them into his [[Dark Dimension]] at will, turning them into [[Mindless Ones]]. **'''Magical Energy Manipulation''': [[File:Dormammu energy blast.png|thumb|250px|Dormammu attacks [[Doctor Strange]]]] Dormammu could use mystical cosmic energies for powerful energy blasts to constantly killed Doctor Strange, albeit with less than successful results. He also summoned tentacle-like entities from the ground to kill Strange, as well as summoning shards of matter to impale him. **'''Telekinesis''': Dormammu used telekinesis in order to throw a moon-like ball of matter at [[Doctor Strange]], which killed him. ===Abilities=== {{Quote|Dormammu deceives you. You have no idea of what he truly is. His eternal life is not paradise, but torment.|The Ancient One to [[Kaecilius]]|Doctor Strange (film)}} *'''Master Tactician''': Dormammu was able to manipulate [[Kaecilius]] and the other [[Zealots]] into doing his bidding by deceiving them all with peace without end, which they all desired. However, once his goal would have been completed, he planned to turn them into [[Mindless Ones]]. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[Clea]] - Niece[https://www.empireonline.com/movies/features/empire-spoiler-special-film-podcast-sign-up/ ''The Empire Spoiler Special Film Podcast''/''Empire Spoiler Specials'': ''Doctor Strange In The Multiverse Of Madness Interview Special ft. Richie Palmer & Michael Waldron''] ===Allies=== *[[Mindless Ones]] - Subordinates *[[Zealots]] - Disciples **[[Kaecilius]] **[[Lucian Aster]] † **[[Blonde Zealot]] **[[Brunette Zealot]] **[[Tall Zealot]] † {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Mephisto]] *[[Masters of the Mystic Arts]] **[[Agamotto]] † **[[Ancient One]] † **[[Doctor Strange|Stephen Strange/Doctor Strange]] - Victim (in the time loops) **[[Wong]] *[[Karl Mordo]] {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|DSPIC}} (glyph) *{{Appearance|DS}} **{{Appearance|AIWTHJ}} (concurrent events) *{{Appearance|AIWP2}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|IH105}} (art & mentioned) *{{Appearance|IH106}} (mentioned) ==Trivia== *In the comics, Dormammu was a [[Faltine]] sorcerer who left his original dimension and became the ruler of the Dark Dimension. He once enlisted [[Baron Mordo]] as a servant to fight the [[Doctor Strange]]. He and [[Odin]] play chess to determine the fate of the Universe, but the games end in a stalemate to maintain the balance between Chaos and Order. *It was [[Benedict Cumberbatch]]'s idea to perform motion-capture for Dormammu, as he felt that the character should be an evil reflection of Doctor Strange.[https://www.ign.com/articles/2016/10/26/13-coolest-doctor-strange-easter-eggs-references-and-trivia?page=2 13 COOLEST DOCTOR STRANGE EASTER EGGS, REFERENCES, AND TRIVIA PAGE 2 OF 2] at [https://www.ign.com IGN] ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Benedict Cumberbatch]] was only credited for portraying [[Doctor Strange]] in the film. The fact that he also portrayed Dormammu was revealed by [[Scott Derrickson]] in an interview with {{WPS|IGN}}. Cumberbatch's voice was blended with another actor's, to make it harder to tell it was him.[http://www.empireonline.com/movies/features/doctor-strange-scott-derrickson/ Doctor Strange: 12 revelations from director Scott Derrickson] at [http://www.empireonline.com Empire] The director said he was unable to recall who the other actor was, only that he was also British.[http://ie.ign.com/articles/2016/10/25/benedict-cumberbatch-plays-a-second-marvel-character-in-doctor-strange BENEDICT CUMBERBATCH PLAYS A SECOND MARVEL CHARACTER IN DOCTOR STRANGE] at [http://ie.ign.com IGN Latam] *The producers of ''[[Doctor Strange (film)|Doctor Strange]]'' asked [[Tony Todd]] to do a recording session for Dormammu, because they wanted another voice as an alternative to Cumberbatch, but they went back to their original choice eventually.[http://www.cbr.com/interview-tony-todd-candyman-the-flash/?utm_source=CBR-TW&utm_medium=Social-Distribution&utm_campaign=CBR-TW&view=list GENRE VET TONY TODD TALKS HIS CAREER, ZOOM AND RETURNING TO THEATER] [http://www.cbr.com CBR.com] *Dormammu had a cameo originally written for ''[[Avengers: Endgame]]'', appearing in [[Hong Kong]] during a sequence where the [[Ancient One]] would show [[Bruce Banner]] what could happen if all the [[Infinity Stones]] were removed from her reality.[http://disneystudiosawards.s3.amazonaws.com/avengers-endgame/Avengers_Endgame.pdf ''Avengers: Endgame'' script by Stephen McFeely and Christopher Markus] ==References== {{Gallery|Dormammu/Gallery}} {{Wq}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP}} *{{Marvel.com|characters/dormammu}} [[es:Dormammu]] [[ru:Дормамму]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Doctor Strange (film) Characters]] [[Category:Book Characters]] [[Category:Mystical Entities]] [[Category:Faltine]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:No Hair]] [[Category:White Eyes]] [[Category:Purple Eyes]] [[Category:High Body Count]]
